title: In a weighted undirected graph, if the length of the shortest pa
content:
In a weighted undirected graph, if the length of the shortest path from `b` to `a` is 12, and there exists an edge of weight 2 between `c` and `b`, then the length of the shortest path from `c` to `a` must be no less than 10.
~@[](3)
author: 陈越
description: In a weighted undirected graph, if the length of the shortest path from `b` to `a` is 12, and there exists an edge of weight 2 between `c` and `b`, then the length of the shortest path from `c` to `a` must be no less than 10.

title: Let P be the shortest path from S to T. If the weight of every e
content: Let P be the shortest path from S to T. If the weight of every edge in the graph is incremented by 2, P will still be the shortest path from S to T.  ~@[](2)
author: 陈翔
description: Let P be the shortest path from S to T. If the weight of every edge in the graph is incremented by 2, P will still be the shortest path from S to T.

title: What kind of problems can Dijkstra algorithm solve?
content:
What kind of problems can Dijkstra algorithm solve? @[](1)

A. critical path
B. shortest path
C. topological sort
D. string matching
author: DS课程组
description: What kind of problems can Dijkstra algorithm solve?

title: Use Dijkstra algorithm to find the shortest paths from 1 to ever
content:
Use Dijkstra algorithm to find the shortest paths from 1 to every other vertices.  In which order that the destinations must be obtained? @[](2)

![Dij1.JPG](~/1f6fb93a-a4ee-4e48-bc0f-b169fe89f890.JPG)


A. 2, 5, 3, 4, 6, 7
B. 2, 4, 3, 6, 5, 7
C. 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
D. 5, 2, 6, 3, 4, 7
author: 陈越
description:
Use Dijkstra algorithm to find the shortest paths from 1 to every other vertices.  In which order that the destinations must be obtained?

![Dij1.JPG](~/1f6fb93a-a4ee-4e48-bc0f-b169fe89f890.JPG)

title: If besides finding the shortest path from `S` to every other ver
content:
If besides finding the shortest path from `S` to every other vertices, we also need to count the number of different shortest paths, we can modify the Dijkstra algorithm in the following way: add an array `count[]` so that `count[V]` records the number of different shortest paths from `S` to `V`.  Then `count[V]` shall be initialized as: @[](3)

A. `count[S]=1; ` and `count[V]=0` for other `V`
B. `count[S]=0; ` and `count[V]=1` for other `V`
C. `count[V]=1` for all vertices
D. `count[V]=0` for all vertices

title: Use Dijkstra algorithm to find the shortest paths from v1 to eve
content:
When Dijkstra's algorithm is used to find the shortest paths from $$v_1$$ to every other vertices in an undirected graph G, a distance set $$dist[v]$$ is maintained for every vertex $$v$$, as the shortest distance from $$v_1$$ to $$v$$, passing through only the vertices whose shortest path to $$v_1$$ has been determined.

Suppose that the destinations are obtained in the order of { $$v_2$$, $$v_3$$, $$\cdots $$, $$v_n$$ }. If there is a vertex $$w$$ in G so that $$dist[w]$$ is decreased during every iteration (except the last one) of the algorithm, how many statements of the following are **correct**?

(1）$$w$$ is adjacent to every other vertices.

(2）($$v_1$$, $$w$$) (if it exists) is the longest edge among all the edges that are adjacent to $$w$$.

(3)  Such a vertex $$w$$ does not exist.
author: 何钦铭
description:
When Dijkstra's algorithm is used to find the shortest paths from $$v_1$$ to every other vertices in an undirected graph G, a distance set $$dist[v]$$ is maintained for every vertex $$v$$, as the shortest distance from $$v_1$$ to $$v$$, passing through only the vertices whose shortest path to $$v_1$$ has been determined.

Suppose that the destinations are obtained in the order of { $$v_2$$, $$v_3$$, $$\cdots $$, $$v_n$$ }. If there is a vertex $$w$$ in G so that $$dist[w]$$ is decreased during every iteration (except the last one) of the algorithm, how many statements of the following are **correct**?

(1）$$w$$ is adjacent to every other vertices.

(2）($$v_1$$, $$w$$) (if it exists) is the longest edge among all the edges that are adjacent to $$w$$.

(3)  Such a vertex $$w$$ does not exist.

title: Find Unweighted Shortest Paths
author: 陈翔
